title: rcicrely user guide
text: reverse correlation (RC), participants’ trial-level choices project onto pool noise patterns reconstruct mental representation used. output per-producer classification image (CI) plus group-level CI obtained averaging. infoVal (Brinkman et al., 2019) tells whether CI contains signal chance. tell whether signal stable across producers, replicable different subset producers, distinguishable another condition’s CI. rcicrely fills gap pixel-level reliability toolkit: Within-condition (consistent condition’s CI across producers?): permuted split-half Spearman-Brown, leave-one-sensitivity, ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k). -condition (condition ’s CI distinguishable condition B’s?): vectorised Welch t per pixel, cluster-based permutation max-statistic FWER, bootstrap representational dissimilarity. Critically, none requires phase-2 trait-rating study. metrics read directly pixel-level signal produced original producers, sidestepping two-phase design Cone et al. (2020) flagged.

Prefer Mode 2 raw responses available. must use Mode 1, package emits one-time warning per session reminding caveat. silence: generated PNGs rcicr::generateCI2IFC(scaling = "none"), rendered output effectively raw, warning genuinely false positive.

title: rcicrely user guide
text: scaling underlying mask. Pearson r per column exactly 1 (linear scaling correlation- preserving), row-mean variance ICC formula depends ~25x larger rendered version. ICC(3,*) computed rendered data therefore ICC computed raw mask. logic applies Euclidean distance infoVal.
code: set.seed(1) n_pix <- 32L * 32L n_p   <- 20L mask  <- matrix(rnorm(n_pix * n_p, sd = 0.02), n_pix, n_p)  # Render: stretch each column to [-0.5, 0.5] (Schmitz "matched") rendered <- apply(mask, 2L, function(m) {   rng <- diff(range(m)); if (rng == 0) m else m * (1 / rng) })  c(   raw_max_range      = round(diff(range(mask)),     3L),   rendered_max_range = round(diff(range(rendered)), 3L) ) #>      raw_max_range rendered_max_range  #>              0.162              1.159  # Pearson r between same-column raw and rendered: ~ 1 # (a perfect linear stretch preserves Pearson) mean(vapply(seq_len(n_p),             function(j) cor(mask[, j], rendered[, j]),             numeric(1L))) #> [1] 1  # But the variance ratios that drive ICC are very different var(rowMeans(mask))      # variance of the group mean over pixels #> [1] 1.923774e-05 var(rowMeans(rendered))  # same metric, on the rendered version #> [1] 0.001134698

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Reads every PNG/JPEG directory, converts grayscale, returns pixels participants numeric matrix. use . one CI image per producer already saved disk want load R numeric matrix. Pair extract_signal() get base-subtracted signal matrix. Reading result. nrow = n_pixels, ncol = n_files. img_dims attribute carries c(nrow, ncol) original image plotting helpers know reshape. Common mistakes. Forgetting subtract base passing rel_* function (use extract_signal() convenience wrapper load_signal_matrix()). Mixing CIs different sizes - aborts loudly. Trusting reliability numbers without reading raw vs. rendered note (chapter 3).
code: # Make a tiny synthetic dataset on disk to demonstrate against. demo_dir <- tempfile("ci_demo_"); dir.create(demo_dir) base_img <- matrix(0.5, 32L, 32L) png_path <- function(i) file.path(demo_dir, sprintf("p%02d.png", i)) if (requireNamespace("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   for (i in 1:6) {     img <- pmin(pmax(base_img + matrix(rnorm(32 * 32, sd = 0.05), 32, 32),                      0), 1)     png::writePNG(img, png_path(i))   } } cis <- read_cis(demo_dir, acknowledge_scaling = TRUE) dim(cis) #> [1] 1024    6 attr(cis, "img_dims") #> [1] 32 32

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Native Brief-RC 12 implementation Schmitz’s genMask() formula. rcicr::*_brief calls (don’t exist canonical rcicr v1.0.1). use . Brief-RC 12 trial-level responses noise pool used stimulus generation. also get rendered CI plotting: Reading result. $signal_matrix always raw mask, regardless scaling. $rendered_ci exists scaling != "none". $participants, $img_dims, $scaling metadata. title: rcicrely user guide
text: rest guide ’ll work small synthetic dataset two conditions different latent signals.
code: set.seed(1) make_condition <- function(region_top, n_pix = 32L * 32L,                            n_p = 30L, snr = 0.3) {   noise <- matrix(rnorm(n_pix * n_p, sd = 0.05), n_pix, n_p)   mask <- if (region_top) {     c(rep(1, n_pix / 2L), rep(0, n_pix / 2L))   } else {     c(rep(0, n_pix / 2L), rep(1, n_pix / 2L))   }   out <- noise + snr * outer(mask, runif(n_p, 0.7, 1.3))   attr(out, "img_dims") <- c(32L, 32L)   out } sig_A <- make_condition(region_top = TRUE,  n_p = 30L) sig_B <- make_condition(region_top = FALSE, n_p = 30L)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Estimates stable condition’s group CI across random halves producers. Reports per-permutation correlation halves Spearman-Brown projection full sample. use . want answer “get group CI ’d run different half participants?”. r_SB usually headline number. Permutation distribution split-half r. Reading result. $r_hh = mean per-split r. $r_sb = Spearman-Brown projected reliability full sample (report unless asked otherwise). $ci_95, $ci_95_sb percentile CIs. $distribution full per-permutation r vector. Common mistakes. Reporting $r_hh instead $r_sb audience cares full-sample CI. Running n_permutations < 200 trusting CIs.
code: r_sh <- rel_split_half(sig_A, n_permutations = 500L, seed = 1L,                        progress = FALSE) print(r_sh) #> <rcicrely split-half reliability> #>   N producers:          30 #>   n_permutations:       500 #>   mean per-split r:     0.993  [0.992, 0.993] #>   Spearman-Brown r_SB:  0.996  [0.996, 0.997] plot(r_sh)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . producer, correlates full-sample group CI group CI computed without producer. Producers unusually low LOO correlation flagged. use . Spotting producers whose CI sits far group pattern. default mean +/- SD; use flag_method = "mad" median +/- MAD rule doesn’t get pulled around outliers tries detect: Per-producer LOO correlation. Reading result. $correlations per-producer vector. $flagged lists ids threshold. $mean_r / $sd_r / $median_r / $mad_r centre/spread rules can compare. $flag_method, $flag_threshold record used. Common mistakes. Treating $flagged “drop producers” - investigate first (Cone et al. 2020 caveat: flagged producer may genuinely atypical mental representation). Cross-check rcicrdiagnostics rule response-coding errors.
code: r_loo <- rel_loo(sig_A, flag_threshold = 2.5, flag_method = "sd") print(r_loo) #> <rcicrely leave-one-out sensitivity> #>   N producers:        30 #>   mean LOO r:         1.000 #>   SD:                 0.000 #>   median LOO r:       1.000 #>   MAD:                0.000 #>   flag rule:          r < mean - 2.50 * SD   (=> r < 1.000) #>   flagged producers:  none r_loo_mad <- rel_loo(sig_A, flag_threshold = 2.5, flag_method = "mad") c(sd_threshold = r_loo$threshold, mad_threshold = r_loo_mad$threshold) #>  sd_threshold mad_threshold  #>     0.9999303     0.9999308 plot(r_loo)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Two-way mixed ICC via direct mean squares. Default returns ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k). Pixels fixed (image grid), participants random (drawn producer population). use . Single-scalar reliability summary. ICC(3,k) “stable group-mean CI?”, ICC(3,1) “informative one producer’s CI?”. also report ICC(2,*) reviewer expects : Reading result. $icc_3_1, $icc_3_k, $icc_2_1, $icc_2_k (latter two NA unless requested). $ms_rows, $ms_cols, $ms_error underlying ANOVA mean squares transparency. $model description string print method shows. Common mistakes. Asking ICC(2,) reviewer expects . ICC(2,) treats pixels random sample pixel population, aren’t. numbers usually close ICC(3,*) high pixel counts model mis-specified - report variants = c("3_1", "3_k", "2_1", "2_k") asked. ICC strongly scale-sensitive. Use raw mask .
code: r_icc <- rel_icc(sig_A, variants = c("3_1", "3_k")) print(r_icc) #> <rcicrely ICC> #>   model:        ICC(3,*) / two-way mixed; pixels fixed, participants random #>   N targets:    1024 pixels #>   N raters:     30 participants #>  #>   ICC(3,1):      0.8788 #>   ICC(3,k):      0.9954 r_icc_full <- rel_icc(sig_A, variants = c("3_1", "3_k", "2_1", "2_k")) print(r_icc_full) #> <rcicrely ICC> #>   model:        ICC(3,*) / two-way mixed; pixels fixed, participants random #>   N targets:    1024 pixels #>   N raters:     30 participants #>  #>   ICC(3,1):      0.8788 #>   ICC(3,k):      0.9954 #>   ICC(2,1):      0.8565 #>   ICC(2,k):      0.9944

text: . Vectorised Welch’s t per pixel two condition signal matrices. use . want raw t-map (e.g. custom plotting, threshold sensitivity analysis) without cluster-level inference. Welch’s t (unequal variances) correct - 2IFC Brief-RC conditions can differ N variance.

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Cluster-based permutation test max-statistic FWER control. Pixels threshold form clusters via 4-connectivity; cluster mass = sum t-values inside; null built stratified label permutation; max-mass approach controls family-wise error. use . “image two conditions differ?” proper multiple-comparison control across pixels. Per-pixel Welch t-map; significant clusters outlined. Reading result. $observed_t raw t-map. $clusters data.frame cluster_id, direction, mass, size, p_value, significant. $pos_labels / $neg_labels integer matrices plotting overlays. $null_distribution exposes per-permutation max masses. Common mistakes. Reading cluster_threshold = 2.0 “p < 0.05” - t-cutoff forming candidate clusters, cluster significance threshold. Significance cluster’s mass relative null. Trusting cluster p-values n_permutations < 1000.
code: clust <- rel_cluster_test(   sig_A, sig_B,   img_dims          = c(32L, 32L),   n_permutations    = 500L,   cluster_threshold = 2.0,   alpha             = 0.05,   seed              = 1L,   progress          = FALSE ) print(clust) #> <rcicrely cluster-based permutation test> #>   N_A = 30, N_B = 30 #>   image dims:        32 x 32 #>   cluster threshold: |t| > 2.00 #>   n_permutations:    500 #>   alpha:             0.05 #>   clusters found:    2 (2 significant) #>  #>  cluster_id direction  mass size p_value significant #>           1       neg -9886  512       0        TRUE #>           1       pos  9807  512       0        TRUE plot(clust)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: . Two scalar dissimilarity metrics two conditions’ group CIs - Pearson correlation Euclidean distance - percentile bootstrap CIs. use . “different two conditions overall?” uncertainty. Pair rel_cluster_test() spatial information. Bootstrap distributions Pearson r Euclidean distance. Reading result. $correlation, $euclidean observed values; $ci_cor, $ci_dist percentile CIs; $boot_cor, $boot_dist full bootstrap distributions. Common mistakes. Reading $correlation near 1 “conditions look ” - identical spatial patterns different magnitudes give r ~ 1; magnitude difference shows $euclidean. $euclidean scales sqrt(n_pixels) within-study metric, comparable across resolutions.
code: diss <- rel_dissimilarity(   sig_A, sig_B,   n_boot   = 500L,   ci_level = 0.95,   seed     = 1L,   progress = FALSE ) print(diss) #> <rcicrely representational dissimilarity> #>   n_boot:     500 #>   CI level:   95% #>   Pearson r   = -0.996   [-0.994, -0.991]   SE = 0.001 #>   Euclidean   = 9.668   [9.293, 10.089]   SE = 0.207 plot(diss)

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt (2024) introduced Brief-RC 12: trial shows 12 noisy faces (6 oriented, 6 inverted, drawn shared noise pool); participant picks one. recorded format one row per trial, columns participant_id, trial, stimulus (chosen pool id), response (+1 oriented, -1 inverted). rcicrely’s implementation native (rcicr _brief functions, don’t exist canonical rcicr v1.0.1). Brief-RC infoVal deferred (canonical implementation either rcicr rcicrely). needs reference distribution matched producer’s trial count, pool size. rel_* reliability metrics depend . hand-roll infoVal Brief-RC today, compute raw mask (res$signal_matrix), res$rendered_ci saved PNG (carry scaling step give wrong reference comparison).
code: # 1. Generate the noise pool (once per project) - heavy step. rcicr::generateStimuli2IFC(   base_face_files     = list(base = "data/base.jpg"),   n_trials            = 600L,        # becomes the Brief-RC pool size   img_size            = 256L,   stimulus_path       = "data/stim",   label               = "mystudy",   ncores              = 1L,          # macOS-safe default   return_as_dataframe = TRUE,        # capture the noise matrix!   seed                = 1L ) |> saveRDS("data/noise_matrix.rds")  # cache for fast loading  # 2. Collect Brief-RC responses in the per-trial format.  # 3. Compute individual CIs. res <- ci_from_responses_briefrc(   responses       = my_brief_rc_responses,   noise_matrix    = readRDS("data/noise_matrix.rds"),   base_image_path = "data/base.jpg",   method          = "briefrc12",   scaling         = "matched"   # also rendered for plotting )  # 4. Reliability assessment. within  <- run_within(res$signal_matrix, seed = 1L) between <- run_between(res_A$signal_matrix, res_B$signal_matrix,                        seed = 1L)  # 5. Save rendered CI to PNG (visualization only - DO NOT use for stats) if (requireNamespace("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   for (j in seq_along(res$participants)) {     img <- matrix(res$rendered_ci[, j], res$img_dims[1], res$img_dims[2])     img <- pmin(pmax(img, 0), 1)     png::writePNG(img, sprintf("plots/p%02d.png", j))   } }

title: rcicrely user guide
text: Sample size. rcicrely aborts 4 producers per condition warns 30 (Cone et al. 2020 recommend N >= 60 stable reliability assessment). N = 10 metrics computable CIs wide. Split-half. Think r_hh ~ 0.3 weak, 0.5 moderate, 0.7+ strong producer-level consistency. r_SB report asked full-sample CI. LOO flagging. flag starting point investigation, verdict. Use flag_method = "mad" SD rule dominated outliers tries find. ICC(3,) vs ICC(2,). Numerically close high pixel counts ICC(3,*) correctly-specified model. Don’t swap reviewer; report variants = c("3_1", "3_k", "2_1", "2_k") pressed. Cluster threshold != p-value. cluster_threshold = 2.0 t-cutoff forming clusters. Significance decided mass relative null. default roughly two-tailed p < 0.05 large df, small N -rejects - inspect t histogram. Raw vs. rendered CIs. package’s reliability math correct given raw signal matrix. Mode 1 (PNGs disk) loads scaling(mask) instead mask; results differ raw responses produce. size difference depends scaling applied PNG-write time. Mode 2 safe path. infoVal correctness. rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC() extracts raw $ci input CI-list internally, canonical 2IFC path safe regardless scaling. pitfall hand-rolled infoVal implementations, Brief-RC, custom code consumes bare matrix, must fed raw mask. Feeding rendered/scaled values silently distorts reference comparison produces wrong significance verdicts.

title: rcicrely — reliability assessment for reverse-correlation CIs
text: Reliability assessment reverse-correlation classification images. Within-condition consistency + -condition discriminability, without second-phase trait-rating study. rcicrely assesses reliability classification images (CIs) produced reverse-correlation experiments. answers questions infoVal doesn’t: signal CI stable, distinguishable comparison condition? Within-condition: permuted split-half Spearman–Brown correction, leave-one-sensitivity, ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k) via direct mean-squares. -condition: vectorised Welch pixel t, cluster-based permutation max-statistic FWER control, bootstrap representational dissimilarity. Supports standard 2IFC (via canonical rcicr) Brief-RC 12 (Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt, 2024; implemented natively). Companion rcicrdiagnostics: diagnostics catches silent data-processing errors CI computation; rcicrely asks whether CIs reliable discriminable cleanly computed.

what: Cluster-based permutation test with max-statistic FWER control — rel_cluster_test
title: Cluster-based permutation test with max-statistic FWER control — rel_cluster_test
text: Pixel-level discriminability test two condition signal matrices. Use identify image two conditions' CIs differ, family-wise error control across pixels. Pair rel_dissimilarity() overall magnitude. Implements Maris & Oostenveld (2007) approach Nichols & Holmes (2002) max-statistic family-wise error control: Compute observed pixel-wise Welch t. Threshold |t| > cluster_threshold (separately positive negative tails); label connected components 4-connectivity. observed cluster, cluster mass = sum t-values cluster (pixel count). Build null distribution via n_permutations stratified label permutations (preserves (N_A, N_B)). , find clusters record max positive mass max negative mass. Observed cluster's p-value = fraction null max-masses (matching sign) equal exceed observed mass absolute value. Stratification (sec.7.5): unstratified permutation lets two group sizes drift, changes Welch degrees freedom null biases max-mass distribution. preserve (N_A, N_B) exactly.

title: Leave-one-out sensitivity — rel_loo
text: Two outlier rules available: "sd" (default): flag producers r_loo < mean(r) - flag_threshold * sd(r). Standard convention; sensitive outliers trying detect. "mad": flag producers r_loo < median(r) - flag_threshold * mad(r). Robust atypical producers often show RC datasets skewed correlation distributions. default flag_threshold = 2.5 calibrated 30-producer dataset flags roughly 0.3 producers chance "sd", rather ~1.5 2-SD rule produce. "mad" roughly comparable thanks MAD's 1.4826 consistency factor.

what: Raw vs. rendered CIs - documentation overhaul + safety net
title: rcicrely 0.1.1 (unreleased)
text: Reliability metrics operate raw mask (participant’s unscaled noise contribution). Reading PNGs disk via read_cis() silently loaded rendered CI (base + scaling(mask)) fed scaling(mask) downstream metrics, can distort variance-based statistics (rel_icc(), Euclidean dissimilarity, pixel_t_test(), rel_cluster_test()). canonical 2IFC infoVal path (rcicr::computeInfoVal2IFC()) unaffected extracts raw $ci element rcicr CI-list internally; hand-rolled infoVal implementations (Brief-RC, custom code) affected need raw mask. mathematical core package always correct; release sharpens boundary users see issue. read_cis(), extract_signal(), load_signal_matrix() now emit -per-session cli warning explaining raw-vs-rendered distinction, metrics affected, silence (acknowledge_scaling = TRUE argument options(rcicrely.silence_scaling_warning = TRUE)). Every rel_*() run_*() function now runs cheap heuristic entry (looks_scaled()) emits -per-session warning input matrix’s dynamic range looks rendered rather raw. Heuristic ; silencing options. vignette("tutorial") restructured function--function user guide (~600 lines, ~350). Adds dedicated “Mental model: signal matrix” chapter numerical demonstration scaling distorts variance-based metrics leaving Pearson correlation intact, “Interpreting results” chapter flags infoVal pitfall. README.md adds top-level “Important: raw vs. rendered CIs” callout.

Mirrors ci_from_responses_2ifc()’s new keep_rendered = TRUE argument, adds $rendered_ci field extracted rcicr’s $combined.

title: rcicrely 0.1.1 (unreleased)

"mad" flags producers whose LOO correlation falls median(r) - flag_threshold * mad(r), robust atypical producers often dominate SD rule. flag_threshold_sd argument renamed flag_threshold (multiplier applies either SD MAD). old name still works alias existing code break. Result object gains $median_r, $mad_r, $flag_method, $flag_threshold fields.

title: rcicrely 0.1.0
text: rel_split_half() - permuted split-half Spearman-Brown correction; handles odd N via per-permutation drop. rel_loo() - leave-one-sensitivity 2.5-SD outlier flagging default. rel_icc() - ICC(3,1) ICC(3,k) via direct mean-squares (two-way mixed model: pixels fixed, participants random); ICC(2,*) available request. pixel_t_test() - vectorised Welch’s t per pixel. rel_cluster_test() - cluster-based permutation test 4-connectivity labelling max-statistic FWER control; permutation stratified (preserves N_A, N_B). rel_dissimilarity() - bootstrap correlation + Euclidean distance percentile CIs (boot dependency). run_within(), run_between() - run every metric, wrap rcicrely_report. read_cis(), extract_signal(), load_signal_matrix() pre-computed CI images. read_noise_matrix() rcicr .Rdata plain-text noise matrices. ci_from_responses_2ifc() - wraps rcicr::batchGenerateCI2IFC() known gotchas handled. ci_from_responses_briefrc() - native Brief-RC 12 implementation per Schmitz, Rougier & Yzerbyt (2024), matching genMask() exactly including duplicate-stimulus tie-breaking. S3 methods - print, summary, plot every rcicrely_* result class plus rcicrely_report.
